item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great SCP, but there's a small thing that bugged me in the writing: Shaw repeats "of course" an inordinate number of times. It's confined to the things Shaw herself wrote, so I assume it was intended to be a character trait of hers, but the repetition was so dense it took me out of thinking "this is how this person writes" and into "this is a character trait the author decided this person has, but went overboard establishing it". I was also bugged by the two paragraphs in a row ending with "Needless to say, […]" but that may have only been because the "of course" thing got me in a repetition-seeking mindset.

item.title: Standing ovations
item.content:encoded:
As a personal victim of abuse, I feel a strong resonance in this piece that, while it still maintains its clinical tone, still manages to strike a chord.
At the beginning, I felt bad for Danton. I expected SCP-3236-A to be an evil entity (like so many before) but you made her so much more than this. You've captured my mind, heart, and soul within this piece and I don't think this ones gonna be leaving me for a long time.
I want nothing more than to send this to publishers to expand further on this. It's just so good.
Keep up the good work and take my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My goodness, the random creative commons guy even looks like the exact asshole Danton is written to be like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is equal parts character piece, short story, and thought-provoking 'example work' for a specific element of worldbuilding, and each part is fantastic. I feel like it suffers a tiny bit from the disconnect between the first and second halves, but this is definitely a case where the whole is more than a sum of its parts, and it more than makes up for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good, liked the story of the Ethics Committee doing its thing. I do have some minor nitpicks about the piece:

+ show block
– hide block

Operation Black Dove. (See Addendum 4263-1.)

I think "Black Dove (see Addendum 4263-1)." looks cleaner.

There are breaks every time you introduce an image due to the way Wikidot joins quoteblocks. You can have them all in one block if you use a [[div]] instead, styled appropriately. (See here).

Mr. Danton begin his work with the Foundation as an agent recruited out of the United States Military,

The US military doesn't really have "agents", "officer" probably makes more sense here. It would also make sense to specify a specific branch, given how large they are and the differences they have in organizational culture.

The previous log was a call from Junior Researcher Alan Raleigh, who had recently been assigned to Site-36. Yes, I know we said it was an anonymous. Yes, that was a lie. We find it helps people find the will to report things.

I mean, I figured this was probably true, but wouldn't this be 5/ETHICS classified? Like confidential informants are a thing; just having the Committee member say what we all expected is a bit jarring. You could keep the dialogue the same by just calling them "John Doe" in-universe for privacy and the narrative effect would be the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I swear to god, you're a writing machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the second time you've written about the Ethics Committee stepping in to put an end to corruption within the Foundation, and the second time I've loved it. This article achieved exactly what was intended; I felt for the girl and absolutely despised the director, and the report had me hooked until the end. Upvoted with enthusiasm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. Very well done story and great scp overall. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed the narrative part. I think my favorite part was the characterization of Danton - he was not only a scumbag, but a scumbag you could imagine actually existing, exploiting the vulnerable for personal gain. It really calls to mind the kind of corruption that exists in the real world. +1

This is absolutely amazing. I really felt for poor Anna and wanted to punch Danton into a different timeline. The concept was also unique and amazing, taking a simple reality bender and mixing her with the greed of a horrible man. Idk once again, amazing!

item.title: 100 skips/pages by the end of the month
Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to murdermenow1200, RadioactiveRADs, Balee and DrLycus for their feedback in chat!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY
